skill.name: langchain-architecture
skill.description: Design LangChain applications with agents, chains, memory, retrieval, callbacks, testing, and production patterns.
skill.summary: Plan LangChain architectures for agentic and retrieval-based LLM applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, weak-cryptography keywords, and a network-reconnaissance phrase. Manual review found these are false positives in documentation and Python examples, with no executable scripts, cryptographic implementation, reconnaissance logic, or prompt-injection text. The skill is low risk because it discusses external tools and APIs that users must implement themselves.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Classified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The listed lines are Markdown inline code or Python fenced code examples in SKILL.md. They are documentation, not Ruby source, and there is no shell command execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct inspection confirms the backticks delimit Markdown code examples. No Ruby files, executable scripts, or command invocation APIs are present in the package.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Non-Crypto Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The matched lines contain a description, LangChain agent identifiers, an initialize_agent call, and a pitfall heading. No weak cryptographic algorithm or crypto API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence lines do not implement or configure cryptography. The static result appears to match substrings inside ordinary documentation or LangChain names.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Matched Tool Access Wording
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 26 says the skill applies to autonomous AI agents with tool access. It does not describe scanning hosts, enumerating networks, or collecting network targets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a use-case bullet for LangChain agents. No commands, network scanner logic, target enumeration, or external reconnaissance workflow appears in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: LangChain projects can become hard to structure when agents, memory, retrieval, and tools interact. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan clear LangChain architectures and implementation patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains core LangChain components, including agents, chains, memory, document processing, and callbacks.
Provides architecture patterns for RAG, custom tools, multi-step chains, and memory management.
Shows Python examples for agent initialization, retrieval chains, callbacks, testing, caching, batching, and streaming.
Identifies common production concerns such as token limits, rate limits, observability, error handling, and prompt versioning.
Helps compare memory strategies for short sessions, long conversations, entity tracking, and semantic retrieval.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include runnable project scaffolding or packaged source files.
Several referenced resources and assets are named in SKILL.md but are not included in this package.
Examples use older LangChain import styles that may require updates for current LangChain versions.
External services such as OpenAI, SerpAPI, email, and vector stores still require user configuration and credentials.

use_cases.title: Plan an Agentic Support Assistant
use_cases.description: Design an agent that routes requests, calls approved tools, keeps useful conversation context, and exposes operational callbacks.

use_cases.title: Structure a RAG Workflow
use_cases.description: Map document loading, chunking, embedding, vector storage, retrieval, and answer generation into a maintainable LangChain design.

use_cases.title: Review Production Readiness
use_cases.description: Check a proposed LangChain application for memory growth, observability gaps, token costs, rate limits, and fallback handling.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Components
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new LangChain application
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the langchain-architecture skill to recommend LangChain components for a simple customer support assistant. Include agents, memory, tools, and callbacks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a RAG Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user building document question answering
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the langchain-architecture skill to design a RAG pipeline for internal policy documents. Cover loaders, splitting, embeddings, vector storage, retrieval, and source reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Review Agent Tooling
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer validating tool access before implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the langchain-architecture skill to review this LangChain agent design. Identify tool selection risks, memory risks, observability needs, and testing strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced team preparing a complex LLM workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the langchain-architecture skill to produce a production architecture for a multi-step LangChain workflow with retrieval, agent tools, streaming, callbacks, tests, and fallback behavior.

output_examples.input: Design a support chatbot that can search documentation and remember the current conversation.
output_examples.output: A layered design with a retrieval chain for documentation, windowed memory for recent turns, tool descriptions for safe routing, and callbacks for latency and token tracking.

output_examples.input: Review an agent that can search a database and send email.
output: Separate read-only search tools from action tools.
Log tool inputs and outputs through callbacks.
Add tests that verify correct tool selection and failure handling.

output_examples.input: Choose a memory pattern for a long-running assistant.
output_examples.output: Use summarized memory for older turns, a short sliding window for recent details, and retrieval memory for durable facts that need semantic lookup.

best_practices: Define each tool with narrow scope, clear descriptions, validation rules, and explicit approval requirements for side effects.
Select memory based on conversation length, privacy needs, retrieval quality, and token budget.
Add callbacks, tests, timeouts, rate limits, and fallback paths before production deployment.
anti_patterns: Giving agents broad tool access without input validation or approval gates.
Keeping unbounded conversation history until context windows or costs fail.
Building retrieval chains without chunking strategy, source tracking, or evaluation tests.

faq.question: Does this skill run LangChain code?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance and examples. Users must create and run their own implementation.

faq.question: Can it help with RAG applications?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers document loading, splitting, embeddings, vector stores, retrievers, and source-aware answer chains.

faq.question: Does it include production-ready templates?
faq.answer: No packaged templates are present. SKILL.md references extra resources, but this package only contains SKILL.md.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users verify before implementation?
faq.answer: Verify current LangChain APIs, service credentials, network permissions, tool safety, and data retention requirements.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found no executable malware, prompt injection, or confirmed dangerous code patterns.
